phase3_findings.description: scripts/discover-deferral-manifests.py committed non-executable (100644) while the fence invokes it as a granted vendored-literal leading token; no interpreter wrapper is available on the cloud tiers, so it would die rc 126 on every run and land in the fail-closed discovery=[failed] arm that reads as legitimate degradation.

The mid-traversal OSError test was vacuous with respect to the onerror=_raise guard it exists to protect: the stub raised unconditionally, exercising the except handler but never the onerror channel. Mutation evidence supplied (onerror=_raise -> os.walk(root) left the suite green).

Module docstring asserted stderr 'always' carries a roots-echo line 'on every run'; the zero-argument usage error (exit 2) returns before it. Propagated to the inline comment and the fence's 'EVERY path' claim.
The exit-2 comment claimed 'the fence must not read it as a failed traversal', which the shipped fence does not honor — exit 2 emits neither marker and lands in the else/failed arm.

VC-24: the marker mutual-exclusivity comment asserted the property 'by construction'; it holds only for callers passing path-safe roots, since the per-root breadcrumb interpolates the root path and the OSError text.
VC-25: the docstring asserted a failed classification covers an OSError 'anywhere inside' the root; the walk is pruned below depth 2, so a depth>=3 subtree is never visited.
The count-locked routing header ('Seven further exits ... first six ... seventh') sits above eight bullets; two reviewers independently read it as an off-by-one.
'absolute resolved path' invites an expectation of symlink resolution; os.path.abspath normalizes only.
classify_root discards matches already collected when an OSError occurs mid-walk (returns ('failed', [])), so manifests found before the error are dropped.
Relocate the cwd-drift bullet into the clean-no-op arm rather than leaving it as a peer bullet.

fix_decisions.claim_text: classify_root should return ('failed', matches) rather than ('failed', []) so manifests found before a mid-walk OSError are not discarded
fix_decisions.evidence: issue #555's exit-contract acceptance criterion specifies exit 4 as 'every root classified failed (empty stdout)'; returning matches from a failed root would let an all-failed run print paths, contradicting a shipped acceptance criterion. The fail direction is already safe: the partial arm files from the clean roots and records a dropped-failed reflection naming the failed root.

parking_evidence.basis: the change would contradict the issue's explicit exit-4 empty-stdout acceptance criterion
parking_evidence.failing_input: an all-failed run that had collected matches before the error would print paths under exit 4

fix_decisions.claim_text: fold the cwd-drift bullet into the clean-no-op arm
fix_decisions.evidence: the ambiguity it caused was resolved directly (F-7) by making the bullet self-label as a heuristic rather than an exit; relocating the bullet would re-open the count-locked header this change just stabilized, for no additional correctness.

parking_evidence.basis: the underlying ambiguity was fixed by F-7; the relocation is cosmetic and would destabilize the count-locked header
parking_evidence.failing_input: a reader miscounting the exits — already addressed by the self-labelling fix

checklist.evidence: holds only for path-safe roots; the breadcrumb interpolates the root path

checklist.claim: failed covers an OSError anywhere inside the root
checklist.evidence: pruned below depth 2; a depth>=3 subtree is never visited
NORMALIZED (wording-only): ntpath.relpath always renders with os.sep; relpath under os.walk can never contain '..'

checklist.claim: cloud-writer closure should include the helper
checklist.evidence: NORMALIZED (wording-only): REQUIRED_HELPER_HEADS is a required subset, not an exhaustive closure; verify passes
NORMALIZED (wording-only): no fence issues set -u, and the routing is answer-independent between no-sentinel and discovery=[]
